text: While you sleep, your brain is filing today's memories for long-term storage. Sleep isn’t just rest; it's a powerful memory processor. Ever wondered how this works? During slow-wave sleep, your brain sorts and stabilizes new memories like a librarian organizing books. Then comes REM sleep, where emotions connected to those memories are intensified, making them more vivid. So, when you dream, your brain isn’t just goofing off; it's solidifying your experiences for the future. Want to unlock the secrets of your brain? Like and follow for more fascinating insights!
